Key claims include rules for choosing weapons, armor, and shields based on strength, skill-based restrictions, and price calculations. Entities touched include weapons, armor, shields, and character skills. Pages written: sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-1-4-character-creation-part-2.

chunks.heading: 2.3 Checks
chunks.notes: Created the main chapter page for 2.3 Checks. Updated the index. No other pages were created or updated as part of this chunk's content.

chunks.heading: 2.7 Resistance Rolls
chunks.notes: Created source page for '2.7 Resistance Rolls' (pages 33-36) in Sword World RPG - Complete Edition. Key claims: life force resistance rolls use adventurer level + life force bonus to avoid physical dangers like poison; mental power resistance rolls use adventurer level + mental power bonus to avoid magic or curses; baseline score equals adventurer level + relevant bonus; characters with level 0 have baseline score of 0. Advised noting baseline scores on character sheets for quick reference. No additional entity/concept pages created beyond the main chapter page.

chunks.heading: 3.4 Movement and Actions
chunks.notes: Created source page for 3.4 Movement and Actions. No other pages written as the PagePlan only authorized the main chapter page. Key claims include movement distances, action restrictions during different movement types, and the three movement options: full, normal, and stand still. Entities touched include agility, movement speed, and combat actions.

chunks.heading: 4.5 Attacks From Characters Against Monsters
chunks.notes: Key claims: Characters attack monsters with a hit check using attack power and 2D, where attack power + 2D ≥ monster's evasion points to hit. If a hit check succeeds, base damage is determined using Table 4-1: Rating Table, with bonus damage added and monster's defense points subtracted. Entities touched: attack power, evasion points, base damage, bonus damage, defense points, critical hits. Pages written: sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-4-5-attacks-from-characters-against-monsters.

chunks.heading: 4.9 Unconscious and Death Checks
chunks.notes: Key claims: Characters fall unconscious when life force reaches 0 or below, and must make death checks. Death checks use life force resistance for adventurers and life point resistance for monsters. Mercy can be declared before an attack to affect death check outcomes. Life force can be recovered through magic, first aid, or natural recovery. Entities touched: Ducard II, Scarnose. Pages written: sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-4-9-unconscious-and-death-checks.

The page outlines the five types of magic (ancient, spirit, holy, dark, dragon roar) and their associated rune masters. No other pages were created in this chunk as per the ingest profile constraints.

chunks.heading: 5.1.15 Holy Magic and the Priest Skill
chunks.notes:
Key claims from the chunk: Holy magic uses runes called holy prayers to contact gods and perform miracles. The skill to use holy magic is called the priest skill, and those with it are called priests. There are restrictions on using the priest skill, including the requirement to have faith and worship only one god. Wicked gods and their followers (dark priests) are not allowed for player characters. The six major gods and several other gods are listed with their teachings. The chunk also covers basic abilities of the priest skill such as divination and vow.

Entities touched: gods, holy magic, priest skill, dark magic, dark priests, six major gods, wicked gods, holy prayers, faith, faith loss, multiple gods worship.

Pages written: sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-5-1-15-holy-magic-and-the-priest-skill (source page)

chunks.notes: Key claims: The merchant skill is a general skill enabling adventurers to trade, with abilities Value Check and Negotiation. Merchants need a pass to conduct public transactions. The hunter skill is an adventurer skill similar to the ranger skill, with overlapping abilities. Craftsmen create items with quality depending on skill level, and healers treat injuries and illnesses. Pages written: sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-7-2-merchant-skill.

chunks.heading: 13.2.6 Mythical Beasts and Magical Beasts (part 1)
chunks.notes: Key claims: Satyrs are forest-dwelling creatures with human upper bodies, goat-like lower bodies, and horns, carrying flutes to manipulate emotions. Ceiling hangers are magical beasts with arms of different sizes and a glue-like lower body, attacking sleeping prey with surprise and strangling them. Harpies have human female heads and bird bodies, with two races: deela and furia, having different characteristics and abilities. Hippocampi are sea creatures resembling horses with fish tails, capable of moving underwater quickly but suffering penalties on land. Medusas are magical beasts in the form of human women with snakes for hair, capable of petrifying humans with their gaze and having poisonous snake attacks. Androscorpions are mythical beasts with human upper bodies and scorpion lower bodies, using spirit magic and having hallucinogenic poison in their tails. Entities created: sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-6-mythical-beasts-and-magical-beasts-part-1 (source summary).

chunks.heading: 13.2.7 Animals (part 1)

chunks.heading: 13.2.7 Animals (part 2)
chunks.notes: Key claims: Grizzlies are generally solitary, except when a mother bear is with her cubs. A bear's special attack is a _bear hug_ which is a technique in which it holds its opponent in its thick arms, squeezing them. Giant ant soldiers are soldier ants that are raised on a special diet to protect the giant ant queen. Their fangs secrete a highly toxic acid. Anyone hit by a giant ant soldier will have their wound burned by the acid and, in addition to normal damage, must make a life force resistance roll, which if they fail, they will suffer damage resulting from a strike power 10 strike roll plus bonus damage 4. Entities touched: Grizzlies, Bear hug, Giant ant soldier. Pages written: sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-7-animals-part-2.
